description_long: This apartment building features a modern, boxy design with a combination of wood paneling and smooth concrete textures on its exterior. Large, rectangular windows are arranged in horizontal bands, allowing ample natural light into the interior spaces. The front facade includes a prominent glass balcony above a set of floor-to-ceiling windows, creating a striking visual contrast. The building appears to have two stories, with the upper floor partially open to the exterior via the balcony. The entrance is marked by a single, dark-colored door set into the concrete section of the facade. The overall aesthetic is clean, geometric, and contemporary, suitable for an upscale urban neighborhood.
description: A modern two-story apartment building with wood and concrete exterior, large horizontal windows, and a glass balcony above a prominent entrance.
description_view_0: This view shows a mix of wood paneling and concrete, with two sets of horizontal windows and a partial view of the balcony railing.
description_view_1: The rear facade is mostly concrete with a vertical split of wood, featuring two long horizontal window bands and no visible entrance.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the main entrance, a large glass balcony, and extensive floor-to-ceiling windows, emphasizing the building's modern style.
description_view_3: This side view displays a mostly wood-paneled wall with vertically aligned windows and a clear view of the balcony's glass railing.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in horizontal bands and vertical groupings, with some asymmetry between the wood and concrete sections. The front features large, floor-to-ceiling glass panels, while the sides have smaller, regularly spaced windows.
